events.title: US Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports
events.desc: The U.S. military has established a naval blockade of Iran's ports and coastal areas following the breakdown of peace talks between the U.S. and Iran. Six merchant ships have reportedly turned back from Iran's ports.

events.title: Iran's Asymmetric Counterair Campaign Strikes US Air Base
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. This is part of a broader counterair campaign waged through asymmetric means.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Scattering Mines over Iranian Village
events.desc: The US appears to have deployed the Gator Scatterable Mine system over Kafari, a village near Shiraz, Iran, resulting in casualties according to Iranian media. Experts suggest the munitions are US-made Gator anti-tank mines.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been leaked online, affecting 12 out of 13 ministries and potentially exposing sensitive information due to basic security vulnerabilities.

events.title: Iran's Internet Blackout During Conflict
events.desc: During Israeli and American strikes, Iran's internet traffic collapsed by 98%, indicating a near-total blackout. The government deliberately dismantled communication infrastructure to halt internet traffic to its own citizens.

events.title: Iranian Propaganda Using AI-Generated Content
events.desc: Sophisticated AI-generated content, described as 'slopaganda,' is being used for propaganda purposes in Iran. Experts highlight its power and effectiveness in shaping narratives.

events.title: Iran's Nuclear Program Set Back, Stockpile Intact
events.desc: While recent attacks by the U.S. and Israel have likely damaged Iran's nuclear and ballistic capabilities, they have not seized its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, which remains a key factor for future negotiations.

events.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Falter, Blockade Imposed
events.desc: Peace talks between the U.S. and Iran have broken down, leading to the U.S. imposing a naval blockade of Iran's ports and coastal areas in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Lebanese and Israeli Ambassadors Begin Direct Talks in the US
events.desc: The ambassadors of Lebanon and the United States have commenced direct talks in Washington, marking the first such dialogue between the two countries in decades. U.S. officials have called it a 'historic opportunity'.

events.title: Italy Suspends Defense Agreement with Israel Amidst Shifting Alliances
events.desc: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni announced the suspension of an automatic renewal of a defense cooperation deal with Israel, signaling a potential shift in Italy's foreign policy concerning the Middle East conflict.

events.title: Israel Continues Strikes on Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Efforts
events.desc: Israel has intensified strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue amid looming peace talks. Over 160 people have been killed in Lebanon since Friday, with Israel pressing its campaign against Hezbollah despite a US-Iran ceasefire.

events.title: Sudan Conflict Continues Unabated
events.desc: Three years into the war in Sudan, millions have been displaced and thousands killed, with no clear end to the ongoing violence in sight. The conflict remains largely misunderstood.

events.title: Munition Remnants at Chad Strike Site Match RSF Weapon
events.desc: Munition remnants from a strike in Tiné, Chad, which killed at least 17 people, appear to match a weapon previously used by Sudan's Rapid Support Forces (RSF), despite RSF denials.

events.title: US-Israel War Exposes Vulnerabilities in Semiconductor Supply Chain
events.desc: The ongoing conflict has highlighted the critical role of bromine, a key component in semiconductor manufacturing. Disruptions to its supply chain, exacerbated by the war, could halt global memory chip production.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Risks Global Food Security
events.desc: A closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, poses a significant risk to global food security due to disruptions in the supply of urea, a critical nitrogen fertilizer produced using energy-intensive processes.
